channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel releases Palestinian who took part in attack at 13 and developed mental illness in prison
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel on Thursday released a Palestinian who took part in an attack when he was 13 and developed schizophrenia in prison as requests for early release were denied. Israel says Ahmad Manasra is a terrorist who tried to kill Jews his own age.
item.dc:creator: MAHMOUD ILLEAN and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sudan faces the world's worst humanitarian crisis as second anniversary of war nears, UN says
item.description: A senior U.N. official says a nearly two-year-old war has engulfed Sudan in the world’s largest humanitarian crisis and led the African country to become the only nation experiencing famine.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democrat says Trump-Xi in 'testosterone battle' with tariff war
item.description: Sen. Jeff Merkley (D-Ore.) on Thursday likened the U.S. tariff war with China to a “testosterone battle” between President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Khalil lawyers blast Rubio court filing that claims power to deport noncitizens over speech
item.description: Mahmoud Khalil’s attorneys on Thursday blasted a court filing from Secretary of State Marco Rubio claiming he has the ability to remove noncitizens for their speech and beliefs, a filing that came after an immigration judge demanded the government present evidence against the former Columbia Univers
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Prince Harry meets war victims in unannounced visit to Ukraine
item.description: Prince Harry has met with war casualties in Ukraine as part of his ongoing work with wounded veterans.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Exclusive: Prince Harry, King Charles' rift widens; Charles refused to see Harry in UK
item.description: Prince Harry also raised eyebrows this weekend when he missed the wedding of close friend, Lord Vivian.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Israel releases 10 Palestinians detained from Gaza. They say they suffered abuse
item.description: Israel has freed 10 Palestinians detained from Gaza, the latest in periodic releases from the thousands of people troops have seized during the military campaign in the territory.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The United States and China are locked in a faceoff over tariffs. No one wants to blink first
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The tariff fight between the world's two largest economies spiraled into greater peril Wednesday as President Donald Trump tried to narrow his global trade war into a direct — and risky — faceoff with Beijing.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Influencer Andrew Tate accused of pointing gun at woman's face ahead of London civil case
item.description: LONDON (AP) — One of four women suing influencer and self-styled misogynist Andrew Tate for sexual assault said he whipped her with a belt and once stuck a gun in her face, according to court documents that have been filed in London.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EU putting tariff countermeasures on hold for 90 days after Trump pause
item.description: The European Union (EU) is putting its retaliatory tariffs on hold for 90 days after President Trump instituted a pause on his sweeping duties for most U.S. trading partners, European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen unveiled early Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Tariffs on China are 145%, White House clarifies as stocks slump
item.description: World markets skyrocketed Thursday in the wake of President Donald Trump's 90-day retaliatory tariff pause.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s Iran talks raise big questions on Capitol Hill
item.description: President Trump is set to open direct talks with Iran this weekend in a high-stakes push for Tehran to give up its nuclear weapons ambitions, raising a chorus of questions and concerns from lawmakers in both parties.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: A prisoner swap between Moscow and Washington frees Ksenia Karelina, a US-Russia dual national
item.description: Russia has freed a Russian American convicted of treason in exchange for a Russian German man jailed on smuggling charges in the U.S. Thursday's prisoner swap comes as the two countries work to repair ties.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Countries will follow Trump imposing tariffs on China: Gordon Chang
item.description: Chang, author of the new book "Plan Red: China's Project to Destroy America," said he expects to see tariffs rise on Chinese goods worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine says more than 150 Chinese mercenaries are fighting for Russia in Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Wednesday expanded on its claim that significant numbers of Chinese nationals are fighting for Russia’s invading army, saying it had gathered detailed intelligence on more than 150 mercenaries Moscow allegedly recruited through social media.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Canadian PM: Negotiations with Trump on economic, security relationship to begin after election
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said Wednesday that negotiations with President Trump on the U.S.-Canada economic and security relationships will start after the Canadian federal election.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump hits back with a 125% tariff in escalating trade war with China
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — President Donald Trump raised the tariff on Chinese imports to 125% on Wednesday, hours after China boosted the duty on American goods to 84% in an escalating battle that threatens to disrupt trade between the world's two largest economies.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 124 dead after roof collapse at Dominican club, officials scramble to ID victims
item.description: Rescue crews from Puerto Rico and Israel arrived Wednesday to help with the search. Officials said crews used sonar to detect what could be one person still breathing under the rubble.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Long, fraught timeline of tensions between Iran and the US as nuclear negotiations approach
item.description: Iran and the United States will hold talks in the sultanate of Oman on Saturday in an attempt to jump-start negotiations over Tehran’s rapidly advancing nuclear program. President Donald Trump insists they’ll be direct negotiations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 8 people die from cholera in South Sudan as funding cuts force longer walks to clinics
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Five children were among eight people who died from cholera in South Sudan after aid cuts are forcing patients to walk for hours to the nearest clinics, an aid organization said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indonesia offers to temporarily shelter wounded and orphaned Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto said Wednesday that his country will offer temporary shelter to Palestinian medical evacuees and children orphaned by the Israel-Hamas war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspected US strikes in Yemen kill at least 8 people, Houthi rebels say
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes pounded the area around Yemen's Red Sea port city of Hodeida on Tuesday night, killing at least eight people, the Iran-backed Houthi rebels said. The strikes hit around Hodeida's al-Hawak district, the rebels said, and wounded 16 people.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Famous North Korean assassin who became a pastor in the South following failed 1968 mission dies
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Kim Shin-jo, a prominent ex-North Korean commando who resettled in South Korea as a pastor after his daring mission to assassinate then South Korean President Park Chung-hee in 1968 failed, died on Wednesday. He was 82.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US, Panama ‘taking back’ canal from ‘China's influence,’ says Hegseth
item.description: Defense Secretary Hegseth said the U.S. and Panama are strengthening security ties and "together" will "take back" the canal from the influence of China, which operates two major ports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 18 dead, 120 injured in roof collapse at Dominican Republic nightclub
item.description: A roof collapse at Jet Set nightclub in the Dominican Republic killed 18 and injured 120. Rescue teams are searching for survivors, while the cause remains under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boris Johnson pecked by ostrich
item.description: Former British Prime Minister Boris Johnson had a fowl encounter with an ostrich during a sight-seeing trip in Texas, with video showing him getting nipped by the bird while his son laughed in his lap.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WGN 9

item.title: Duke basketball star could be deported under Trump visa policy
item.description: The State Department announced Saturday that visas of South Sudanese passport holders would be revoked.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says US will deal 'directly' with Iran in high-level meeting on Saturday
item.description: President Trump announced that the U.S. will engage directly with Iran on nuclear negotiations on Saturday though he would not comment on how the talks will differ from JCPOA agreements.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US revokes visas for South Sudanese as civil war looms at home
item.description: Here’s a look at South Sudan, whose people had been granted temporary protected status by the U.S. because of insecurity at home. That status expires on May 3.
item.dc:creator: CARA ANNA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas terror outlet quietly cuts Gaza death count, reveals most killed were combat-age men
item.description: New analysis shows thousands of names were removed from the Gaza casualty list as Hamas terror organization admits most deaths aged between 13 to 55 were males.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope appears in St. Peter's Square for first time in weeks
item.description: Pope Francis greeted crowds in St. Peter's Square on Sunday, his first public appearance since being discharged from the hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN global comms arm under fire for anti-Israel bias as critics call for reforms
item.description: The U.N.'s Department of Global Communications is under fire for a one-sided view of the Israel-Palestinian conflict, with critics saying it often sides with Palestinians at the expense of other conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sick sea lions, other mammals stranded on beaches in Baja, Southern California
item.description: Officials are reporting an unusually high number of beached animals due "domoic acid or phytotoxin" in the water.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Officials say a Russian strike in central Ukraine kills 14 people, including six children
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian ballistic missile strike Friday on a central Ukrainian city killed at least 14 people, including six children, Ukrainian officials said, as U.S. and European leaders pressed Russia to accept a ceasefire in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Nintendo delays Switch 2 US preorders over Trump tariffs
item.description: Nintendo said U.S. preorders of its Switch 2 console will not take place on April 9 as it assesses tariff implications.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strike on a school in Gaza kills at least 27 people, Palestinian health officials say
item.description: DEIR AL BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes killed at least 100 Palestinians across the Gaza Strip on Thursday, including 27 or more sheltering at a school, according to Palestinian medical authorities, in a stepped-up offensive that Israel’s military said is intended to pressure Hamas and e
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, WGN 9
